description_long: This is a large, futuristic airplane with a streamlined fuselage and a sharply pointed nose, suggesting high-speed capability. The wings are mid-mounted and swept back, each supporting two large cylindrical engines, for a total of four engines, which are likely jet or turbofan types. The tail section features a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers. The landing gear is tricycle-type, with a visible nose wheel and main gear under the wings or fuselage. The cockpit is positioned at the very front of the fuselage and appears to be enclosed, with a canopy integrated into the nose section. The exterior is primarily metallic gray, giving it a modern, military or advanced civilian aircraft appearance.
description: A large, metallic gray, swept-wing airplane with four underwing engines, a pointed nose, and a conventional tail. The cockpit is forward-mounted and enclosed, with tricycle landing gear visible.
description_view_0: Rear view showing twin exhausts, swept wings with four large engines, and the tail section with a single vertical stabilizer.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the pointed nose, forward cockpit, mid-mounted swept wings, and the tricycle landing gear with the nose wheel extended.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the sharp nose, forward cockpit canopy, and the symmetrical arrangement of engines under the wings.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile similar to view 1, showing the same features from the other side, including the landing gear and engine placement.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing, swept-back wings with no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles; four underwing engine mounts.
attribute_engine_type: Four underwing jet or turbofan engines, two per wing.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with one nose wheel and two main gear units, likely retractable.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit integrated into the nose section, likely single or multi-seat with a streamlined canopy.
